findings.title: Crude oil prices soar on Middle East conflict escalation
findings.summary: Crude oil prices surged amid fears of prolonged Middle East war disrupting supply. This exacerbates global energy inflation pressures under active tariff regime, challenging central bank efforts to stabilize prices.
findings.claim_excerpt: Crude oil prices soared as investors worried about the potential duration and intensity of the conflict in the Middle East.
findings.signal_type_basis: Medium-term supply shock from geopolitics amid tariff-induced inflation, likely persisting weeks to months.
findings.preliminary_assessment: Energy shock reinforces inflationary regime, pressuring bond yields higher and complicating CB easing.
findings.confidence_basis: Multiple sources corroborate oil surge and market reaction tied to Middle East tensions.

sources_cited.excerpt: Crude oil prices soared as investors worried about the potential duration and intensity of the conflict.
Escalating Iran tensions and potential U.S. military involvement; Market impacts from disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz.

search_queries_run: Middle East conflict oil prices April 2026
research_traceback.analyst_confidence_notes: Direct alignment across financial press and market analysis on energy supply risks.

findings.episodic_reason: Geopolitical event-driven spike, monitor for 3+ week sustainment.

cross_monitor_relevance.erm: AI capex/energy implications: higher energy costs strain data center builds
cross_monitor_relevance.scem: Commodity/EM stress: oil shock hits import-dependent EMs

findings.review_reason: Assess if energy shock sustains beyond episodic into CC inflation driver.

findings.title: Sovereign bond yields spike across US, Europe, Japan
findings.summary: Yields on major sovereign bonds rose sharply due to inflation fears, policy tightening risks, and deficits. China bonds buck trend with falling yields, attracting global capital.
findings.claim_excerpt: Sharp increase in the yields on bonds issued by many major countries including the United States, those of Western Europe, and Japan.
findings.signal_type_basis: Reflects medium-term inflation and policy shift expectations amid energy/tariff pressures.
findings.preliminary_assessment: Signals hawkish repricing in fixed income, potential headwind to growth.
findings.confidence_basis: [AUTO-DOWNGRADED from High: single source at Tier 0 — Analyst cron to verify] Corroborated by detailed yield movement analysis in primary source.

sources_cited.excerpt: Sharp increase in the yields on bonds issued by US, Europe, Japan; China yields fallen.

research_traceback.analyst_confidence_notes: Single detailed source but comprehensive coverage justifies High; needs secondary confirm.

cross_monitor_relevance.esa: Rising EU yields signal fiscal strain under tariff coercion

findings.review_reason: Track if yield spike embeds as structural inflation signal.

findings.title: US services PMI contracts to 49.8 amid price pressures
findings.summary: US services PMI fell to 49.8 in March, first contraction since 2023, blamed on rising prices and uncertainty from tariffs/Middle East war. Signals broader economic buckling.
findings.claim_excerpt: The PMI for the US services industry fell from 51.7 in February to 49.8 in March... buckling under the strain of rising prices and intensifying uncertainty.
findings.signal_type_basis: Cyclical slowdown in services amid persistent inflation, weeks/months horizon.
findings.preliminary_assessment: Early warning of demand softening under inflation, tariff uncertainty amplifier.
findings.confidence_basis: PMI data directly quoted with S&P Global context.

sources_cited.excerpt: US services PMI 49.8, first decline since Jan 2023; strain from prices, tariffs, war.
Central banks look through global energy supply shock but marginally hawkish guidance.

research_traceback.analyst_confidence_notes: PMI figure and drivers aligned across sources.

cross_monitor_relevance.scem: EM equities: US slowdown risks global demand hit

findings.title: Eurozone inflation accelerates, ECB rate hikes priced in
findings.summary: Eurozone inflation picked up, with markets pricing ECB rate hikes in 2026 due to energy shocks. Futures indicate 1-2 increases by year-end.
findings.claim_excerpt: Eurozone inflation accelerates... futures market pricing ECB benchmark rate raise one or two times before end of 2026.
findings.signal_type_basis: Medium-term policy response to accelerating core inflation from energy.
findings.preliminary_assessment: Divergent CB paths (ECB hawkish vs potential Fed pause) widen yield spreads.
findings.confidence_basis: [AUTO-DOWNGRADED from High: single source at Tier 0 — Analyst cron to verify] Clear market pricing tied to inflation data.

sources_cited.excerpt: Eurozone inflation accelerates; ECB hikes priced.

cross_monitor_relevance.esa: European fiscal autonomy strained by hawkish ECB

findings.title: Bank of Japan upgrades CPI and GDP forecasts
findings.summary: BOJ raised headline/core CPI and real GDP forecasts for current/next years amid sticky inflation. Signals end of yield curve control potential.
findings.claim_excerpt: Bank of Japan positively revised its headline CPI, core CPI, and real GDP growth forecasts.
findings.signal_type_basis: Cyclical inflation persistence driving policy normalization over months.
findings.preliminary_assessment: Global hawkishness convergence pressures JGB yields higher.
findings.confidence_basis: Single source but detailed forecast revisions.

sources_cited.excerpt: BOJ positively revised CPI and GDP forecasts for current/next fiscal years.

research_traceback.analyst_confidence_notes: Transcript-based; requires official BOJ confirm for upgrade.

findings.title: FOMC upgrades core PCE and GDP forecasts
findings.summary: Median FOMC member raised 2026-27 core PCE and real GDP estimates, signaling sticky inflation and productivity boost. Supports no further cuts narrative.
findings.claim_excerpt: Median FOMC member positively revised 2026,2027 core PCE forecasts; GDP estimates up.
findings.signal_type_basis: Medium-term inflation stickiness amid productivity gains.
findings.preliminary_assessment: Hawkish Fed dot plot reprices Treasuries, aligns with global trend.
findings.confidence_basis: Detailed dot plot analysis from transcript.

sources_cited.excerpt: FOMC positively revised GDP, unemployment down, core PCE up for 2026-27.

research_traceback.analyst_confidence_notes: Single source; cross-check official Fed release needed.

findings.title: China 10Y yields fall as US/EU/JP yields rise
findings.summary: China's 10Y yield declined modestly post-Middle East conflict, driven by high savings and investor flight to safety. Contrasts with inflation-driven rises elsewhere.
findings.claim_excerpt: China... yield on the 10-year bond has fallen modestly since the conflict began.
findings.signal_type_basis: Episodic safe-haven flow amid crisis, not sustained trend.
findings.preliminary_assessment: Highlights capital shift to China amid G3 inflation fears.
findings.confidence_basis: [AUTO-DOWNGRADED from High: single source at Tier 0 — Analyst cron to verify] Specific yield divergence documented.

sources_cited.excerpt: China 10Y yield fallen; contrasts US/EU/JP rises on inflation fears.
